Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Of Defendant Swinerton Builders
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF TODD KOERLIN'S Motion To Tax Costs Of Defendant Swinerton Builders is granted. Plaintiff has shown that there is a sufficient unity of interest between Swinerton and Herreron to warrant apportionment of costs between them. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Quash Third Party Records Subpoena
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF TODD KOERLIN'S Motion To Quash Third Party Records Subpoena is off calendar at the request of the moving party. Judge Pro Tem: Naomi Gray, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Continued To Mar-21-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Behalf Of Swinerton Innovative Solutions, Llc
Off Calendar Dec-16-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Scotto of The Law Offices of Vincent J. Scotto, III telephone number 650-401-6161 for Plaintiff.
Gregory Thomas and Michael Biggans of Boornazian Jensen & Garthe telephone number 510-834-4350 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SWINERTON INNOVATIVE SOLUTIONS, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that at most Swinerton retained general safety authority at the worksite and did not affirmatively contribute to plaintiff's injury. Herrero undertook the job of infilling the pans with the wooden infills. (Tate Declaration paragraphs 9-10; Lefler Depo 16:21-25). Swinerton never promised to take a particular safety measure regarding the pans. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR #12913. (302/HK)
